label: warm_P5.3
answer:
Hi Soham,
Your last cholesterol reading (April 5, 2026):

Total Cholesterol: 237 mg/dL
LDL: 162 mg/dL
HDL: 38 mg/dL
Triglycerides: 186 mg/dL

These numbers show your cholesterol is higher than ideal — your LDL (the "bad" cholesterol) is elevated, and your HDL (the "good" cholesterol) is low. This increases cardiovascular risk, which is why you were referred to a cardiologist.
Imaging from 2026:

CT Scan - Paranasal Sinuses (March 12): Showed chronic rhinosinusitis in your maxillary and anterior ethmoid sinuses
Chest X-ray (January 8): Normal — no cardiopulmonary issues

You also had an ECG (April 5) which showed normal sinus rhythm.
Your next appointment with your doctor is scheduled for February 18, 2026. This would be a good time to discuss your cholesterol levels and the cardiologist's recommendations.
Not medical advice — talk to your doctor before acting on this.
